pattern: Divide and conquer on the first character missing its opposite case: it cannot be inside any nice substring, so split there and recurse.
hints: If a character appears in only one case within the whole string, no nice substring can contain that position.
Find any such offending character; every nice substring lies entirely to its left or right, so split the string at it and solve each side.
Recurse on the pieces and keep the longest result, preferring the earliest one on ties; a string with no offending character is itself nice.

edge_inputs.description: nice run inside a larger string
longer later candidate but earliest equal-length wins

stress.description: random mixed-case string over a small letter set so nice substrings appear
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    pool = 'aAbBcCdD'
    return [''.join(rng.choice(pool) for _ in range(n))]
